title: HubSpot (HUBS) Announced Q4 2025 Earnings on Feb. 11, 2026, Reporting Full Year Revenue of $3.1 Billion
description:
HubSpot (HUBS) reported financial results for the period, noting that "full year 2025 revenue grew 18.2% to $3.1 billion". For the fourth quarter, "Q4 revenue grew 18% year over year in constant currency, and 20% on an as-reported basis". Management highlighted profitability, stating "operating margin of 22.6% in Q4" and "Net income was $163,000,000, or $3.09 in Q4". Additionally, "Free cash flow was $290,000,000, or 25% of revenue in Q4".
In terms of business updates, the company "added 9,800 n

title: HubSpot Earnings FY2025 Q1｜HubSpot Goes All-In on AI — But Is 102% NRR a Warning Sign?
description:
In this episode of SaaS Japan Unlocked, we break down HubSpot’s FY2025 Q1 earnings — a quarter that blends solid growth, strong free cash flow, and the first real steps toward AI-native monetization.
With 258,000+ customers and over 660,000 Copilot users, the numbers look strong on the surface — but a 102% NRR and declining ARPU raise important questions.
We unpack:
Why NRR 102% may actually be underwhelming
What’s holding back the Rule of 40
How ARR growth is still acquisition-led
The role of s

title: Overheard at HubSpot INBOUND 2025: The new AI marketing playbook explained
description:
Janet and Tucker share a recap of the HubSpot INBOUND 2025 conference, sharing honest opinions and observations about the conference itself, announcements shared by HubSpot, and the overall future of marketing. They also analyze HubSpot’s new Loop Marketing playbook, defining it as a critical framework for growth in the AI era.

In this episode, you’ll get actionable insights on:

♾️ Analysis of HubSpot’s Loop Marketing framework: The four stages—Express, Tailor, Amplify, and Evolve—are explored

title: Elad Gil Explains Why 90% of Startups Fail in Every Tech Cycle
description:
Elad Gil, of Gil Capital and Gil & Co, aka one of Silicon Valley’s most influential investors, sits down with Molly O’Shea to unpack it all.. from the 1999 IPO boom to the AI bubble, and what it really takes to build durable companies across cycles.

Elad has backed more than 200 companies, from Stripe, Airbnb, and Coinbase to next-gen AI leaders like Perplexity, Harvey, and Decagon. Today, he runs Gil Capital, a multi-stage investment firm potentially managing “billions,” and continues to be on

title: To automate or not to automate: How to combine technology with the human touch
description: In this episode of Called to Action, we're diving deep into all things marketing automation! 🤖✨ Should you automate or keep a human touch in your marketing strategy? Or should you aim for a healthy balance of both? Our hosts Janet Mesh and Tucker Delaney-Winn break down key takeaways from HubSpot's 2024 INBOUND conference, including how to balance automation, AI, and human creativity across email marketing, paid advertising, and more. Plus, discover when and how to leverage automation within the

title: How NextRoll and HubSpot Power Smarter B2B Advertising with AI and Account-Based Targeting
description:
In this episode of Up Your Stack, Noah Berk talks with Vibhor Kapoor, Chief Business Officer at NextRoll, the company behind RollWorks and AdRoll. Vibhor shares how their AI-driven platform delivers full-funnel, multi-channel advertising for B2B brands—and how their deep, bidirectional HubSpot integration unlocks high-impact targeting and attribution.
